theory.intro: Breaking a number into factors lets you re-group a multiplication into easier steps. This uses the **associative property**.
facts: You can multiply factors in **any order or grouping**: $a \times b \times c = (a \times b) \times c$.
Look for factors that pair to make $10$, $100$ or another round number.
steps: Factorise one number to expose a helpful factor.
Re-group so an easy product (like $\times 100$) is formed.
